bib.title: On sorting strings in external memory
bib.citation: Proceedings of the twenty-ninth annual ACM symposium on Theory of computing …, 1997
bib.author: Lars Arge and Paolo Ferragina and Roberto Grossi and Jeffrey Scott Vitter
bib.abstract: In this paper we address for thejirst time the 1/0 complexity of the problem of sorting strings in external memory, which is a jimdamental component of many large-scale text applications. In the silznabd unit-cost RAM comparison model, the complexity of sorting K strings of total length N isEl (K logz K+ N). Byanalogy, inthe external memory (or 1/0) model, where the internal memory has size M and the block transfer size is B, it would be natural to guess that the 1/0 complexity of sorting strings is@(~~ ogMIB~+~), but the known algorithms do not come even close to achieving this bound. Our results show, somewhat counterintuitively, that the 1/0 complexi~ of string sorting depends upon the length of the strings relative to the block size. Wefirst consider a simple comparison 1/0 model, where one is not allowed to break the strings into their characters, and we show that the 110 complexity of string sorting in this model is …
